title: 〜そうだ (Hearsay)
description: The hearsay 〜そうだ reports information you heard or read from another source — "I hear that…" / "they say that…" It attaches to the plain form and is often paired with 〜によると ("according to").

content.text: Hearsay 〜そうだ passes along information you got second-hand — from the news, a friend, a weather forecast. It attaches to the plain form of verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives (with だ), and nouns (with だ): 降（ふ）るそうだ, 高（たか）いそうだ, 静（しず）かだそうだ, 学生（がくせい）だそうだ. Unlike opinions, it stays neutral — you're just relaying what you were told, not judging whether it's true.

content.japanese: plain form ＋ そうだ　（＝ I hear that … ／ they say …）
天気予報（てんきよほう）によると、明日（あした）は雨（あめ）だそうです。
content.english: According to the forecast, it's going to rain tomorrow.

content.japanese: 彼（かれ）はもう来（こ）ないそうだ。
content.english: I hear he's not coming anymore.

content.japanese: あのレストランはとてもおいしいそうです。
content.english: I hear that restaurant is really good.

content.japanese: 会社（かいしゃ）はもっと人（ひと）をクビにするそうです。
content.english: I hear the company is going to lay off more people.

content.text: Marking the Source with 〜によると
To name where the information came from, put 〜によると ("according to") at the front of the sentence. It pairs naturally with hearsay そうだ, forming the common 〜によると〜そうです frame.

content.japanese: ニュースによると、トランプさんは怒（おこ）っているそうです。
content.english: According to the news, Trump is angry.

content.japanese: ゆかさんによると、週末（しゅうまつ）日本（にほん）で地震（じしん）があるそうです。
content.english: According to Yuka, there's going to be an earthquake in Japan this weekend.

content.text: Warning: Hearsay そう vs Appearance そう

This is the single biggest trap with そうだ: it has two completely different meanings, told apart only by where it attaches. Plain form ＋ そうだ is hearsay ("I heard"). But stem ＋ そうだ is appearance ("it looks like"), based on what you can see right now. 雨（あめ）が降（ふ）るそうだ means "I heard it's going to rain"; 雨（あめ）が降（ふ）りそうだ (verb stem 降（ふ）り) means "it looks like it's about to rain." Same word, opposite basis of knowledge — always check the attach point.

content.japanese: plain 降（ふ）るそう ＝ I heard　／　stem 降（ふ）りそう ＝ it looks like

examples.japanese: plain form ＋ そうだ　（＝ I hear that … ／ they say …）
天気予報（てんきよほう）によると、明日（あした）は雨（あめ）だそうです。
examples.english: According to the forecast, it's going to rain tomorrow.

examples.japanese: 彼（かれ）はもう来（こ）ないそうだ。
examples.english: I hear he's not coming anymore.

examples.japanese: あのレストランはとてもおいしいそうです。
examples.english: I hear that restaurant is really good.

examples.japanese: 会社（かいしゃ）はもっと人（ひと）をクビにするそうです。
examples.english: I hear the company is going to lay off more people.

examples.japanese: ニュースによると、トランプさんは怒（おこ）っているそうです。
examples.english: According to the news, Trump is angry.

examples.japanese: ゆかさんによると、週末（しゅうまつ）日本（にほん）で地震（じしん）があるそうです。
examples.english: According to Yuka, there's going to be an earthquake in Japan this weekend.

examples.japanese: plain 降（ふ）るそう ＝ I heard　／　stem 降（ふ）りそう ＝ it looks like

Warning: Hearsay そう vs Appearance そう
